rationale: The citing paper gives Barasch and Berger (2014) a specific theoretical role: it is used as evidence that audience features can shift WOM valence via motivational processes (impression management). The authors then position their own work as adding another social-interaction driver, interpersonal closeness, to explain when positive versus negative WOM is shared. The cited paper is also referenced in the discussion as part of the core prior stream on situational and personal determinants of WOM valence. This is more than a passing background citation.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the Barasch and Berger (2014) line of work by moving from audience size to interpersonal closeness as a key audience-related driver of WOM valence. It proposes and tests a different motive-based mechanism (self-enhancement vs protecting others) and shows consequences for valence distortion across transmission chains.
